metric_value: CIS industry experiencing growth nearing 50% per year; leading firms exceeding this
notes: Aberdeen's market research on CIS growth

metric_value: Revenues increasing over 100% on annualized basis; 1H 1996 revenues exceeded $20.1M; Aberdeen expects more than double in 2H 1996
notes: 100%+ revenue growth rate at time of study

metric_value: Aberdeen research indicates Clarify has achieved 99% uptime with its customer base
notes: Aberdeen field research confirming reliability claim

metric_value: Over 200 customers; 43,000 individuals using Clarify solutions
notes: Scale evidence for market leader claim

metric_value: 20% of Clarify's revenues invested in R&D
notes: Signals sustained technology investment above industry average

metric_name: CIS evaluation criterion 1: Speed and Scalability
metric_value: Response time paramount; rapid deployment; global scale for thousands of users
notes: Factor 1 of 5 from Aberdeen's ongoing CIS user interviews

metric_value: Must adapt to company's unique policies/processes; end-user modifiable without IS staff; upgrades preserve customizations
notes: Factor 2 of 5 from Aberdeen's ongoing CIS user interviews

metric_name: CIS evaluation criterion 3: Expertise and product richness
metric_value: Supplier must understand best practices; each application must stand on its own right
notes: Factor 3 of 5; explains why Clarify acquired Metropolis rather than building SFA internally

metric_value: Three-level integration: (1) cross-CIS app data sharing, (2) enterprise back-office (SAP, Oracle), (3) cross-organizational boundaries
notes: Factor 4 of 5; foreshadowed CRM platform integration requirements

metric_value: Remote/mobile access, Internet availability, security, data replication and synchronization for mobile users and customers
notes: Factor 5 of 5; Metropolis acquisition directly addressed this

metric_value: Metadata layer sits atop application logic; ClearCustomize toolset; new version deployments don't corrupt customizations; contrast with source-code modification vendors
notes: Presaged modern SaaS 'configuration not customization' architectural principle

metric_value: Presentation, application logic, database layers partitioned into separate modules dynamically deployed on different hardware
notes: Standard architectural pattern that became universal in enterprise software

metric_value: Clarify elected to acquire rather than build SFA; recognized that robust SFA requires deep sales methodology expertise and mobile computing — both from Metropolis
notes: Strategic acquire-vs-build decision; led to ClearSales and enhanced ClearLogistics

metric_value: Integrated best-of-breed CIS suite; refuse implementations unlikely to succeed; TAM assigned to every customer; linchpin account strategy in IT/telecom verticals
notes: Aberdeen endorses this strategy as producing 99% uptime and consistent deal execution

metric_value: Aberdeen expects Clarify will maintain its rapid growth, particularly in IT and telecommunications markets; 'Clarify is a CIS winner and will continue to be so in the future'
notes: Aberdeen's strong endorsement

metric_value: Clarify acquired by Nortel Networks in 1999 for $2.1B in stock; validating Aberdeen's 'CIS winner' assessment
notes: Source: CNET/Nortel; $2.1B acquisition validated Clarify's market leadership position

metric_value: Nortel sold Clarify to Amdocs for $200M in 2001 — a fraction of the $2.1B paid; CRM market shifted toward Siebel and Salesforce
notes: Source: Computerworld/Amdocs; Clarify's technology was absorbed into Amdocs telecom billing suite

metric_value: ClearSupport CommCenter for telecom: simultaneous customer account + network infrastructure access; proactive customer notification; customers MCI, Sprint, TeleNordia
notes: July 1996 announcement; one of first industry-specific CIS applications Aberdeen predicted would proliferate

metric_value: Market quickly consolidating from hundreds of software suppliers to a few leaders with resources for Fortune 1000 requirements
notes: Aberdeen predicts consolidation benefiting Clarify as a scale leader

metric_value: CIS/CRM market consolidated rapidly: Siebel Systems dominated by 2000, followed by SAP CRM, Oracle Siebel, and eventually Salesforce.com (2000 IPO)
notes: Aberdeen correctly predicted consolidation; Clarify was absorbed rather than becoming a standalone leader

metric_value: Named linchpin accounts: Microsoft, Cisco, HP (IT), MCI, Sprint (telecom), Bay Networks (networking); these customers' partners naturally adopt Clarify
notes: Aberdeen endorses linchpin strategy as a natural pull-through mechanism

metric_value: Clarify's relationship with Microsoft is a 'tremendous resource for helpdesk excellence' — large percentage of helpdesk inquiries are Microsoft application-related
notes: Aberdeen's value of Microsoft as anchor customer for cross-company helpdesk adoption

metric_value: ClearTelesales expected mid-1997: closed-loop sales from marketing campaign management to lead generation, quotation, closure, and forecasting
notes: Product roadmap item; closes Clarify's telemarketing/telesales gap

metric_value: Aberdeen believes Clarify's vision of connecting helpdesks across company boundaries is on-track with market realities; endorsed via CSC and DMTF participation
notes: Foreshadowed modern ServiceNow-style enterprise service management integration
